name: Paysend Global Money Transfer API
what_it_does: Paysend global money transfer and B2B payment REST API for businesses to send international transfers, process multi-currency B2B payments, and enable card-to-card transfers across 150+ countries with direct card network rails for fast cross-border payment automation. Enables AI agents to manage international money transfer initiation for cross-border payment automation, handle card-to-card global transfer for instant international payment automation, access multi-currency B2B payment for enterprise cross-border automation, retrieve transfer status tracking for payment confirmation automation, manage business account and wallet management for corporate treasury automation, handle batch payment processing for bulk international disbursement automation, access FX rate retrieval for currency conversion automation, retrieve payment reporting and reconciliation for international financial automation, manage payroll disbursement for global workforce payment automation, and integrate Paysend with enterprise ERP, HR, and treasury platforms for end-to-end international payment automation.
use_cases: Sending international remittances from cross-border payment agents
Processing B2B cross-border payments from enterprise treasury agents
Disbursing global payroll from international workforce payment agents
Executing card-to-card transfers from instant global payment agents
not_for: Domestic-only payments (Paysend is cross-border focused; use Stripe for domestic)
High-volume complex FX hedging (use Banking Circle or CurrencyCloud for enterprise FX)
Consumer-facing retail POS (Paysend is transfer and B2B focused)
best_when: A business wanting AI agents to automate international money transfers, B2B cross-border payments, and global payroll disbursements across 150+ countries through Paysend's direct card network rails.
avoid_when: TRANSFER CORRIDOR COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS: Paysend cross-border transfers must comply with source and destination country AML/KYC regulations; automated transfer must provide sender and receiver KYC information; automated transfer without required KYC creates compliance rejection for regulated corridors. B2B API ACCESS REQUIRES SEPARATE AGREEMENT: Paysend B2B API is separate from consumer product; automated B2B payment integration requires Paysend Business API agreement; automated consumer API use for B2B payment creates feature limitation. TRANSFER STATUS ASYNC POLLING: International transfers are asynchronous (processing through banking networks); automated transfer confirmation must poll transfer status; automated immediate confirmation after initiation creates premature success status before correspondent banking processing. FX RATE LOCK TIME WINDOW: Paysend FX rates are quoted with time-limited locks; automated payment must execute within rate lock window; automated rate quote use after lock expiry creates rate changed error requiring new quote.

auth.notes: Paysend uses API key and OAuth 2.0 for API authentication. REST API with JSON. London, UK HQ. Founded 2017 by Ronnie Millar and Abdul Abdulkerimov. Backed by One Peak, Hermes GPE ($125M raised). Products: Consumer P2P transfers, B2B payments, multi-currency wallets, card-to-card. 150+ countries. GDPR. FCA authorized. EMI licensed. Competes with Wise and Remitly for global transfers.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — business account registration required
pricing.paid_tiers: Fixed fee per transfer ($1-4 depending on corridor) plus FX spread (0.5-1.5%)
pricing.notes: London UK. FCA authorized. Fixed fee plus FX spread per transfer. Business account required.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; transfer and wallet APIs throttled; webhooks for transfer status events

known_agent_gotchas: SENDER AND RECEIVER KYC REQUIREMENTS: Paysend B2B transfers require sender and receiver identity information (name, address, document); automated transfer must collect and submit KYC data per corridor requirements; automated transfer without required KYC fields creates compliance rejection for regulated corridors

TRANSFER QUOTE vs TRANSFER EXECUTION TWO-STEP: Paysend requires quote (with locked FX rate) then transfer execution; automated payment must complete both steps within rate lock window; automated transfer without prior quote uses real-time rate without lock guarantee

CORRIDOR AVAILABILITY CHECK BEFORE TRANSFER: Not all currency corridors are available at all times (bank processing hours, holidays); automated transfer must check corridor availability; automated transfer to temporarily unavailable corridor creates transfer queuing without clear status

TRANSFER PURPOSE CODE REQUIREMENT: Some corridors require transfer purpose code (business payment, payroll, etc.); automated transfer must include purpose code for required corridors; automated transfer without purpose code creates compliance rejection for purpose-required corridors

WEBHOOK DELIVERY RELIABILITY: Paysend webhook delivery has variable reliability; automated transfer status management must implement polling backup; automated webhook-only status management creates missed status updates for failed webhook deliveries
agent_readiness.security_notes: International transfers. GDPR, FCA authorized. API key/OAuth. GB. Transfer and recipient data.

security_score_components.security_notes: International transfers. GDPR, FCA. API key. GB. Transfer and KYC data.
